evidence_rea: These are for your convenience so that you do not have to fill in your details again when you leave another comment.
evidence_reg: Paragraphs 1, 2 and 3 shall not apply where and insofar as the data subject already has the information.
per_reg_comparisons.reasoning_short: The REA describes convenience in not re-entering details for another comment, while the REG concerns when certain information duties do not apply; they do not clearly express the same or directly conflicting constraint.

evidence_rea: These are for your convenience so that you do not have to fill in your details again when you leave another comment.
evidence_reg: Where requests from a data subject are manifestly unfounded or excessive, in particular because of their repetitive character, the controller may either:
per_reg_comparisons.reasoning_short: The REA is about convenience for repeat commenting, whereas the REG addresses how a controller may respond to manifestly unfounded or excessive requests; no same or conflicting constraint is stated.

evidence_rea: These are for your convenience so that you do not have to fill in your details again when you leave another comment.
evidence_reg: Paragraph 1 shall not apply if one of the following applies: (e) processing relates to personal data which are manifestly made public by the data subject;
per_reg_comparisons.reasoning_short: The REA discusses not having to fill in details again for another comment, while the REG creates an exception for processing data made public by the data subject; there is no clear textual match or conflict.
